howItWorks.intro: This balance transfer calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Interest Saved = Sum of monthly interest at old rate minus transfer fee
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is a balance transfer?
faq.answer: Moving debt from a high-interest card to one with a lower or 0% intro APR. You pay a transfer fee (typically 3-5%) but save on interest during the promotional period.

faq.question: Is a balance transfer worth it?
faq.answer: If you can pay off the balance during the 0% period, yes. The transfer fee is usually much less than the interest you would have paid.

faq.question: What happens after the promo period?
faq.answer: The regular APR kicks in (often 18-25%). Any remaining balance accrues interest at this higher rate. Plan to pay off the full balance before the promo ends.

faq.question: Does a balance transfer hurt my credit?
faq.answer: It may cause a small, temporary dip from the hard inquiry. However, lower utilization on the old card can improve your score over time.

faq.question: Is the Balance Transfer Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Balance Transfer Calculator?
sections.body: The Balance Transfer Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your current balance, current apr, transfer fee, you get instant results including transfer fee, monthly payment, interest saved. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting transfer fee right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your current balance and need to find the right transfer fee. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Current Balance ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your current balance — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: current apr, transfer fee, 0% apr period. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your transfer fee, monthly payment, interest saved. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your current balance before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both transfer fee and monthly payment — they work together to give you the full picture.
